name: Mexican Sausage Stew
recipeIngredient: 1 pound beef brisket, cut into chunks
1/2 pound hot sausage, cut into chunks
1 1/2 pounds sweet potato, peeled and sliced into 1-inch chunks
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat oil in a large Dutch oven.
2. Working in batches, sauté brisket and sausage until browned, about 10 minutes. Transfer to plate.
3. Add onions and garlic to drippings in pot, cook until tender, about 8 minutes, being careful not to burn the garlic.
4. Stir in cinnamon and allspice, cook for 1 minute.
5. Return meat and sausage to pot. Reserve 1/4 cup beef broth.
6. Stir in remaining broth, beer, and water. Cover and simmer until meat is tender, about 1.5 hours.
7. Slice potatoes into 1-inch chunks and cook until tender, about 15 minutes. Stir in corn, heat through.
8. Whisk flour and reserved 1/4 cup broth in a small bowl. Stir into pot.
9. Cook stirring until thickened.
10. To serve, ladle stew into bowls. Top each serving with 2 tablespoons cheese. Garnish with cilantro and accompany with tortilla chips.
